experiments.hypothesis: Redirecting to a relevant tool page instead of generic /features should recover click-through from users searching for barcode/QR scanning tools

experiments.before: The Open Source Asset Tracking Platform | Shelf Asset Management
experiments.after: Open Source Asset Management Software — Free for Teams | Shelf Asset Management
experiments.hypothesis: 8,248 monthly impressions for 'asset management software' with 0.0% CTR. Current title says 'Asset Tracking' not 'Asset Management' and doesn't mention 'free'. Matching the search intent + adding 'free' should lift CTR.

experiments.before: Track equipment across people, locations, and workflows with QR-first simplicity, custody logging, and modern workflows.
experiments.after: Track who has what, where it is, and when it's due back. QR scanning, custody logs, and booking calendars — free for individuals, open source.
experiments.hypothesis: 5,372 impressions at 0.2% CTR and pos 30.9. More conversational tone + mentioning 'free' and 'open source' in the meta description should make the listing more clickable and differentiate from paid competitors.

experiments.after: Equipment Check-In & Check-Out Software — QR Scan, Track, Return | Shelf
experiments.hypothesis: 6,877 impressions at 0.4% CTR and pos 19.0. Adding action verbs (scan, track, return) makes the listing more compelling and concrete in search results vs the generic current title.

experiments.before: No page exists — /macrs-depreciation-calculator redirects to generic /tools
experiments.after: Build dedicated interactive MACRS depreciation calculator tool page
experiments.hypothesis: 8,320 monthly impressions going to a generic /tools page. Building a dedicated calculator should capture this traffic and feed users into the Shelf funnel via CTAs.

experiments.before: No page exists — /equipment-depreciation-calculator redirects to generic /tools
experiments.after: Build dedicated interactive equipment depreciation calculator tool page
experiments.hypothesis: 7,494 monthly impressions going to generic /tools. Dedicated page with straight-line depreciation calculator should capture this traffic.

experiments.before: No page exists — /salvage-value-calculator redirects to generic /tools
experiments.after: Build dedicated interactive salvage value calculator tool page
experiments.hypothesis: 8,163 monthly impressions going to generic /tools. Dedicated calculator page should capture this high-volume traffic.

experiments.before: Snipe-IT Alternative — Modern Open-Source Asset Management | Shelf
experiments.after: Snipe-IT vs Shelf — Which Open Source Asset Manager Fits Your Team? (2026)
experiments.hypothesis: 1,522 impressions at 0.9% CTR, pos 10.0. The 'vs' format with a year tag performs better for comparison queries — makes the listing feel like a current, objective comparison rather than biased marketing.
